problemStatement: Capture agent-made decisions across the design-specify, plan-build, and execute-write cycle in a simple searchable record format that preserves knowledge for future-me and informs future agent-level planning.

EVID-1.statement: The decision-record persistent store at docs/chester/decision-record/decision-record.md is empty after four Chester sprints since shipping 2026-04-24; only .gitkeep present.

EVID-2.statement: Thirty-five decision-making points exist between design brief handoff and end of execute-verify-complete; fourteen of them are captured only as inline conversational prose with no persistent artifact trace.

EVID-3.statement: StoryDesigner's docs/chester/decision-record/ directory does not exist; the project has not used the decision-record store. Cross-sprint memory there is served entirely by LBD design briefs plus master-plan version history plus TDR codification, with 77% of 44 sampled decisions across 11 sprints classified substantive (future-planning useful).

EVID-4.statement: Existing decision-record MCP machinery (dr_capture, dr_supersede, dr_query, dr_audit, append-only store with proper-lockfile concurrent-write protection) is functional and tested via the integration suite; the building blocks are reusable for any new capture pattern.

EVID-5.statement: The current dr_capture 11-field schema requires non-empty values for spec_update (an AC ID), test (a test name), and code (a file:line reference); none of these have natural values at design-specify or plan-build decision moments.

EVID-6.statement: Commit 96ea360 on 2026-04-24 added the trigger-check + propagation step, the observable-behaviors implementer artifact, and the spec-reviewer input-restriction module to execute-write; the implementer + spec-reviewer + quality-reviewer per-task dispatch chain pre-existed this commit and is not part of the additions.

EVID-7.statement: The reasoning audit written by finish-write-records reconstructs four-to-twelve decision points per sprint with context, alternatives considered, decision, rationale, and confidence; the format maps almost exactly onto the existing dr_capture schema fields.

EVID-8.statement: Across StoryDesigner's threat-report samples, approximately 95% of plan-attack and quality-reviewer findings are routine execution hygiene rather than substantive architectural catches; substantive findings tend to also surface at the execute-verify-complete suite gate as broken behavior.

RULE-1.statement: Records are written as markdown in docs/chester/decision-record/.

RULE-2.statement: Capture format is agent-focused — structured machine-queryable fields rather than narrative prose; future agents are the primary readers.

RULE-3.statement: Store is append-only / continuous-write; existing records are never rewritten.

RULE-4.statement: Supersession is by reference — a new record references the prior record, the prior record stays intact, and the newer record is authoritative over the older.

RULE-5.statement: Capture covers agent-made decisions across the design-specify, plan-build, and execute-write cycle — not just one stage.

RULE-6.statement: Keep the solution simple — minimum viable shape that meets the goal.

RULE-7.statement: Revert execute-write to its pre-04-24 shape: drop the trigger-check and propagation step, drop the observable-behaviors artifact, drop the spec-reviewer input-restriction module, drop the propagation-procedure and test-generator references; restore implementer + spec-reviewer + quality-reviewer per-task chain.

RULE-8.statement: Capture serves two altitudes of value: preserving knowledge for future-me (the human reading later) and informing future agent-level planning (the machine querying later).

RULE-9.statement: The decision-record system will not use an MCP. Capture is implemented via direct file operations at the skill level, not via tool calls to a server.

RULE-10.statement: The decision-record system works cooperatively alongside session-level artifacts, not as a replacement. Both surfaces can contain duplicate information. Session artifacts serve session-internal recall ('what did this sprint do'); decision records serve cross-sprint trend-finding ('what's been decided about X across sessions') where reading every prior session's artifacts is impractical.

RULE-11.statement: The decision-record system does not participate in the TDD write loop. Capture is not a gate on test-first / implement / test-pass discipline; execute-write's build cadence runs uninterrupted by record writes.

RULE-12.statement: Capture scope spans the entire session including design-large-task and design-small-task when run in-session, not just the design-specify, plan-build, execute-write subset.

RULE-13.statement: Decision records and audit entries are parallel outputs over the same JSONL source. Each surface has an independent discrimination filter — a decision selected by one filter need not be selected by the other. Filters can be tuned independently across the two surfaces.

NCON-1.statement: Decision records are emitted during the reasoning-audit pass in finish-write-records, derived from the same JSONL transcript source the audit reads.
NCON-1.collapse_test: If records are emitted earlier in the cycle (per-decision mid-stage or stage-close per skill), capture either fragments across multiple skill points, requires agent memory rather than transcript source, costs more tokens per decision, or duplicates and fights the audit's already-validated discrimination logic.
NCON-1.reasoning_chain: IF the audit already discriminates substantive decisions from a complete-session JSONL source, AND capture must avoid TDD-loop participation, AND capture must span the full session cycle, AND records must not use an MCP, THEN piggybacking emission on the audit pass uses an existing validated discrimination mechanism, fires outside the build cadence, naturally covers all stages in one pass, and writes via direct file I/O at finish time.
rejected_alternatives: Per-decision emission at each substantive decision moment within design-specify and plan-build — rejected because it requires agent memory or new skill steps at every decision point, costs tokens proportional to decision count, and would duplicate the audit's discrimination judgment in real-time without the transcript source.

Stage-close emission at end of design-specify and end of plan-build — rejected because it fragments capture across multiple skill points, partial coverage compared to the audit's whole-session reach (which already includes design-large-task / design-small-task), and relies on session memory rather than authoritative JSONL transcript.

NCON-2.statement: Decision records and reasoning-audit entries are parallel outputs over the same JSONL source with independent discrimination filters; a decision included in one need not be included in the other. This implements the cooperative-coexistence framing of RULE-10 (revised): both surfaces can carry overlapping content but each is tuned to its own altitude (records to cross-sprint trend-finding, audit to session-internal recall).
NCON-2.collapse_test: If records strictly mirror audit selections (filters coupled), small precedent-setting decisions that aren't session-recall-relevant are lost from the cross-sprint corpus, and the system cannot tune cross-sprint relevance separately from session-recall relevance.
NCON-2.reasoning_chain: IF the audit serves session-internal recall AND records serve cross-sprint trend-finding (per revised RULE-10), AND the two altitudes have different relevance criteria such that a minor decision can be session-noise yet cross-sprint-precedent (RULE-13 designer constraint), THEN the filters must be decoupled; coupling them strips the freedom to tune each surface independently and forces records to inherit the audit's session-altitude discrimination.

NCON-3.statement: Records live as appended entries in a single markdown file at docs/chester/decision-record/decision-record.md; new records append, prior records are never modified.
NCON-3.collapse_test: Per-file-per-record breaks the single grep-able corpus property and forces directory-listing conventions. Mutating priors to mark supersession breaks the append-only invariant and loses the historical decision trail.
NCON-3.reasoning_chain: IF the corpus is a single queryable cross-sprint surface, AND append-only is invariant, AND the existing path is already established by prior design, THEN a single file at the canonical path supports grep-based query without directory traversal, preserves all priors intact, and matches the established convention.

NCON-4.statement: Each record carries structured fields enabling agent query via grep/parse without LLM judgment — minimum: id, date, sprint, stage, title, decision, rationale, alternatives, tags, supersedes, artifact_refs.
NCON-4.collapse_test: Without structured fields, query becomes LLM-judgment-mediated parsing across prose; tag drift, missing IDs, and unstructured supersession links make cross-sprint discoverability unreliable and force every query to load and re-judge the entire corpus.
NCON-4.reasoning_chain: IF future agents query the corpus mechanically (grep, awk, jq-style frontmatter parse), AND the format must remain simple, AND the audit's existing format already provides most of the substance fields (title, context, alternatives, decision, rationale, confidence), THEN the record field set is the audit format adapted with cross-sprint affordances added (id for stable reference, sprint and stage for filter axes, tags for query axes, supersedes for chain navigation, artifact_refs as cooperation handle to session-level artifacts).

NCON-5.statement: The decision-record-specific additions from the 2026-04-24 build-decision-loop merge are removed surgically from the affected files: the chester-decision-record MCP package, the trigger-check + propagation step in execute-write, the observable-behaviors artifact and spec-reviewer input-restriction modules, the dr_query and must-remain-green additions in plan-build, the AC-N.M skeleton manifest in design-specify, the dr_verify_tests step in execute-verify-complete, the dr_audit step in finish-write-records, the skeleton-generator / propagation-procedure / test-generator reference files, the related bash test scripts, and the .claude-plugin/mcp.json registration. Every change that landed after the merge — provenance stamping, named subagents, pluggable Understanding-MCP, automatic ground-truth review, brief-to-spec AC seeding, Session Skill Versions harvest, heuristic execution-mode selection, cluster-a Resolve Conditions — is preserved.
NCON-5.collapse_test: If the revert is non-surgical (revert the merge), every later improvement is also lost — provenance stamping, named-subagent fork policy, pluggable Understanding-MCP, ground-truth automation, etc. If the revert is partial (some DR pieces remain), dangling references appear (dr_audit in finish-write-records pointing at a removed MCP, must-remain-green field referencing a non-existent dr_query) and the system breaks at integration boundaries.
NCON-5.reasoning_chain: IF the design reverts to the pre-04-24 baseline (RULE-7), AND no MCP is allowed (RULE-9), AND no TDD-loop participation is allowed (RULE-11), AND post-04-24 changes carry independently-validated improvements that do not participate in the DR system or its forbidden patterns, THEN every DR-specific addition from the 04-24 merge is removed and every later change is kept; the revert is surgical (selective line and file edits) rather than a wholesale merge-revert which would also drop the keep-bucket changes.

NCON-6.statement: Finish-write-records gains a new emission step that, during the same agent pass producing the reasoning audit, writes structured records to docs/chester/decision-record/decision-record.md via direct file append, using the structured field set per NCON-4 and applying its own discrimination filter independent of the audit's filter (per NCON-2 revised — parallel outputs over the same JSONL source, each with independent discrimination).
NCON-6.collapse_test: Removing the emission step leaves the rules and conditions in place but no mechanism executes them; the system describes the right capture pattern but never produces a record.
NCON-6.reasoning_chain: IF capture fires at audit time (NCON-1), AND filters are independent across the two surfaces (NCON-2 revised), AND records live append-only at the canonical path (NCON-3), AND records carry structured fields (NCON-4), THEN the audit-emission step is a concrete step in finish-write-records that performs the file write; without this step, the rules and conditions describe a system that has no implementation surface and capture never fires.

RISK-1.statement: Discriminator inheritance: the audit's discrimination logic (the four-to-twelve non-trivial-decision filter) is the source the records' filter shares (parallel filter over the same JSONL transcript, per NCON-2 revised). If both filters miss a substantive decision, records miss it too. Mitigation is the parallel-filter rule (NCON-2) which permits a more inclusive records filter, but the records filter is itself agent-judgment-driven and can inherit the same blind spots at its own altitude.

RISK-2.statement: Tag drift without validator: agent-written records carry tags as a key query axis (NCON-4), but no MCP validates the tag set (RULE-9 forbids server-side validation). Across sprints, an agent might write 'arch' where a prior sprint wrote 'architecture', breaking grep-based queries that filter on tag values. Mitigation is documenting a small canonical tag list inside the writing skill plus existing-tags-first discipline, but discipline is the only enforcement.

RCON-1.statement: Records are emitted at finish-write-records during the reasoning-audit pass, sharing the JSONL transcript source. No mid-stage capture, no MCP, no TDD-loop participation. The capture moment is post-build, retrospective from the transcript.

RCON-2.statement: Each record carries structured fields enabling agent query without LLM judgment: id, date, sprint, stage, title, decision, rationale, alternatives, tags, supersedes, artifact_refs. The format adapts the existing reasoning-audit entry shape and adds cross-sprint affordances (id, sprint, stage, tags, supersedes, artifact_refs).

RCON-3.statement: Records live as appended entries in a single markdown file at docs/chester/decision-record/decision-record.md. Future agents query via grep / awk / jq-frontmatter parse over the single corpus. Supersession is discovered by scanning forward for records whose supersedes field names an earlier record's id; older records are never modified.

RCON-4.statement: All decision-record-specific additions from the 2026-04-24 build-decision-loop merge are removed surgically: the chester-decision-record MCP package, the trigger-check + propagation step in execute-write, the observable-behaviors implementer module, the spec-reviewer input-restriction module, the dr_query and must-remain-green additions in plan-build, the AC-N.M skeleton manifest in design-specify, the dr_verify_tests step in execute-verify-complete, the dr_audit step in finish-write-records (replaced by the new emission step), the skeleton-generator / propagation-procedure / test-generator reference files, the related bash test scripts, and the .claude-plugin/mcp.json registration. Every change landed after the merge is preserved — provenance stamping, named subagents, pluggable Understanding-MCP, automatic ground-truth review, brief-to-spec AC seeding, Session Skill Versions harvest, heuristic execution-mode selection, cluster-a Resolve Conditions.

concerns.label: Capture trigger placement
concerns.description: Where in the design-specify, plan-build, and execute-write cycle does a decision get recorded? At end of each stage, mid-stage, on each substantive decision, or only at sprint close?

concerns.label: Record shape
concerns.description: What fields and structure does an agent-queryable record carry? Reuse the existing eleven-field schema with adaptation, slim to a smaller design-fit set, or introduce phase-typed records?

concerns.label: Cross-sprint discoverability
concerns.description: How do future agents find relevant records when planning a new sprint? Through the existing dr_query, through prior-art-explorer reading the markdown directly, through plan-build's prior-decisions section, or through some new mechanism?

concerns.label: Execute-write revert scope
concerns.description: What exactly is removed when execute-write reverts to its pre-04-24 shape, and what dependent integrations (execute-verify-complete's verify_tests gate, finish-write-records' audit line) need to adjust?
